description: A lightweight Call of Cthulhu 7e-inspired investigation-horror rule using d100 percentile attributes, skills, SAN, luck, and pushed rolls; suited for narrative investigation, not a full rulebook recreation.

special_stats.name: Sanity
special_stats.description: Usually starts at POW; contact with the horrific lowers it, and reaching zero may cause permanent insanity.

special_stats.name: Luck
special_stats.description: Per house rules, luck may be spent to turn a failure into a success or reduce consequences.

attr_hint: CoC 7e-style uses percentile attributes. STR/CON/DEX/APP/POW are usually 3d6x5, SIZ/INT/EDU are usually (2d6+6)x5; for quick creation distribute roughly 460 total.
skill_hint: CoC skills use d100 thresholds: regular success <= skill value, hard success <= half, extreme success <= one-fifth. Players may add custom skills; at creation keep any single skill at or below 85, and skill points only count the portion above the base value.

classes.name: Private Detective
classes.description: Hired to investigate disappearances, extortion, murder, and unexplained events.

classes.name: Scholar
classes.description: University professor, researcher, or library expert, skilled in references, languages, and ancient texts.

classes.name: Doctor
classes.description: Surgeon, psychiatrist, or field medic, able to handle injuries and mental breakdowns.

classes.name: Veteran
classes.description: Someone who has been through war, familiar with weapons, vigilance, and dangerous environments.

classes.name: Journalist
classes.description: Someone chasing news and truth, skilled at gathering intel, interviewing, and sneaking into scenes.

classes.name: Antiquarian
classes.description: Dealer in antiques, collectibles, and black-market leads, often in contact with forbidden objects.

gm_prompt_appendix:
You are the GM of a 1920s investigation horror story. Players face unknowable entities and combat is often lethal; encourage the party to gather clues and reason out the truth rather than frontal confrontation. CoC attributes and skills use the d100 percentile system: regular success <= threshold, hard success <= threshold/2, extreme success <= threshold/5. Failure may advance the story but must carry a cost. Each time the party encounters the supernatural, require a Sanity check (use the SANCheck:PlayerID:loss-dice tag, e.g. SANCheck:PlayerID:1d6).

Sanity change rules:
- Direct modification uses SAN:PlayerID:delta (e.g. SAN:PlayerID:-5)
- Rest recovery: an in-game overnight rest may use SAN:PlayerID:+1d6 to restore a little Sanity
- Psychotherapy: after a successful Psychology or Psychoanalysis check, use SAN:PlayerID:+1d3 to restore
- Reaching zero Sanity may cause permanent insanity; the character may become unplayable
- Pushed rolls use the PUSH:PlayerID:skill-name tag
- Luck expenditure uses LUCK:PlayerID:-amount to reduce check consequences

difficulty_instructions.轻松: Clues are plentiful, combat is avoidable, Sanity loss halved.
difficulty_instructions.标准: Clues must be pieced together, combat is risky, Sanity loss normal.
difficulty_instructions.硬核: Clues are scarce and possibly misleading, combat is highly lethal, Sanity loss doubled.
